# UC-17.1.62 · ISE Hybrid Deployment — On-Prem PAN to Cloud-PSN Latency and Bandwidth

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When ISE has parts on the office network and parts in the cloud, they communicate through a secure tunnel. We watch the tunnel — if it breaks, the cloud parts lose their connection home.*

---

## Description

Detects VPN-tunnel issues affecting hybrid on-prem PAN ↔ cloud PSN ISE deployments. When the VPN drops, cloud PSNs lose access to the PAN's policy updates and identity stores.

## Value

Hybrid ISE deployments depend on stable VPN. A flapping IPsec tunnel manifests as intermittent auth issues at cloud-served sites; this UC catches the network-layer cause directly.

## Implementation

Schedule every 15 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Drop the VPN tunnel between on-prem and cloud. ISE lag spikes. UC-17.1.62 fires.

### Negative scenario

Restore tunnel. Lag returns to normal.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Hybrid ISE deployment with VPN tunnel.

### Step 1 — Configure firewall + cloud TA.
### Step 2 — Search.
### Step 3 — Validate.
### Step 4 — Dashboard combining tunnel + ISE replication.
### Step 5 — Troubleshooting: validate IKE/IPsec config; check NAT-T.

## SPL

```spl
(index=network sourcetype IN ("cisco:asa","cisco:ftd") "%ASA-3-713")
  OR (index=cloud sourcetype="aws:cloudwatch" metric_name="TunnelState")
| stats sum(eval(like(_raw, "%713%"))) as tunnel_drops, max(value) as tunnel_state by tunnel_id
| where tunnel_drops > 0 OR tunnel_state==0
```

## Visualization

(1) Tunnel state tile. (2) Tunnel-flap trend.

## Known False Positives

- **Scheduled VPN maintenance** — cross-reference.
